domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
In the context of a play, is it always associated with at least one author, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A play can exist without any author.
2. A play must have at least one author.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A play can exist without any author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A play must have at least one author.
questions.selected: Option 2: A play must have at least one author.

questions.question:
In the context of a play, can there be more than one author involved, or is it always associated with just one?
For example:
1. A play is linked to a single author.
2. A play is linked to multiple authors.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each play is always linked to a single author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple authors.
questions.selected: Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple authors.

questions.question:
In the context of a participant, is it always associated with at least one play, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A participant can exist without any play.
2. A participant must have at least one play.

questions.option_1: Option 1: A participant can exist without any play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A participant must have at least one play.
questions.selected: Option 2: A participant must have at least one play.

questions.option_1: Option 1: A play can exist without any participant.

questions.question:
In the context of a play, can there be more than one participant involved, or is it always associated with just one?
For example:
1. A play is linked to a single participant.
2. A play is linked to multiple participants.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each play is always linked to a single participant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple participants.
questions.selected: Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple participants.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple participants.

This option is better because the domain description states that plays involve participants, and the same participant may act in multiple plays, implying that multiple participants can be involved in a single play.

questions.question:
In the context of a participant, is it always association with at least one author, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A participant can exist without any author.
2. A participant must have at least one author.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A participant can exist without any author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A participant must have at least one author.
questions.selected: Option 1: A participant can exist without any author.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: A participant can exist without any author.  
A participant can act in multiple plays and is not required to be an author of any play, as the domain description does not state that participants must be associated with authors.

questions.question:
We know that each theater has a location. When we think about location, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is location made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each location, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter theater by detail.
2. The location is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the location.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the location details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for location is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for location is enough.

questions.question:
Should the location in theater be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use location as string attribute in theater, which allows any text value.
2. Use location as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: the northwest theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The location can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The location must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The location can be any text value.

questions.question:
There are different types for plays: musical and opera. Do these types have distinct characteristics or requirements?
For example:
1. Musical and opera are considered different types, allowing for distinct details for each type.
2. Every play type is classified as either musical or opera. All types have the same details, and there’s no need for extra information.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Musical and opera do have distinct characteristics.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Musical and opera doesn't have distinct characteristics.
questions.selected: Option 1: Musical and opera do have distinct characteristics.

questions.question:
Do the concepts (participant and author) represent different kinds of a more general concept called person?.

For example: 
1. Participant and author are unrelated concepts, with no connection to person.
2. Participant and author are different kinds of person.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep participant and author as unrelated concepts.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Create the concept person, with participant and author as its kinds.
questions.selected: Option 2: Create the concept person, with participant and author as its kinds.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Create the concept person, with participant and author as its kinds.  

The domain description indicatea a hierarchical relationship between participants and authors with general concept called "person".

questions.question:
The concept session potentially represents a link between plays and theaters. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. A session is a concept and a link that connects a play and a theater. 
2. A session is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a play and a theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The session is a concept and a link between a play and a theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The session does not represent a link between a play and a theater.
questions.selected: Option 1: The session is a concept and a link between a play and a theater.

questions.question:
Thinking about play, and its kinds: musical or opera. Is it possible to have a play that is not one of those specific types?

For example:
1. There are some plays that are not part of the kinds musical or opera.
2. Every play must be of a specific kind musical or opera.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Some plays exists that is not of the kind musical or opera.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Every play is of a specific kind musical or opera.
questions.selected: Option 2: Every play is of a specific kind musical or opera.
